title: To Live and Die in L.A.
tech: Fuji A125T 8511 · Arriflex 35 BL3 · Zeiss prime lenses · 35mm spherical 1.85:1
writeup: To Live and Die in L.A., 1985. Directed by William Friedkin, shot by Robby Müller on Fuji tungsten stock with an Arriflex. Müller had spent years shooting in Europe with Wim Wenders and brought what Friedkin called "a great foreigner's eye" to the West Coast. He wasn't shooting clichés. His instinct for where to put the camera and where to draw the frame was so precise that the compositions feel found rather than made. The color is not stylized. That's just what the world looked like. Friedkin later said he came "very close to my vision of it in the execution."
writeup2: Nine scene presets built from the actual negative. Pick a preset, pick a processing layer, add your subject. The rest is already in the film.

presets.name: Industrial LA exterior
presets.text: Robby Müller, To Live and Die in L.A. 1985, 35mm spherical 1.85:1, Arriflex 35 BL3, Fuji A125T 8511 tungsten negative. Long telephoto 200mm+, power lines and palm silhouettes compressed into flat graphic lattice. Dead purple-gray smog haze sky. Color drained near monochrome in midtones, warm rust only in low foreground. Camera locked off, surveillance feel. Mid-day sun, no golden hour. Anti-glamour urban realism, observational distance.
presets.neg: No golden hour, no romantic sunset, no warm glowing light, no blue sky, no beauty lighting, no Instagram aesthetics, no HDR, no oversaturation, no sharp digital clarity, no modern color grade, no studio lighting, no fill light, no glamour, no clean edges.

presets.name: Street level observational
presets.text: Robby Müller, To Live and Die in L.A. 1985, 35mm spherical 1.85:1, Arriflex 35 BL3, Fuji A125T 8511 tungsten negative. Fluid dolly or pan tracking subjects through crowd. Strong late-afternoon sidelight from left, subjects slightly underexposed against it. Background signage reads brick-red and faded, never saturated. Documentary grammar, European eye on American street life. No fill light. The world is already lit.
presets.neg: No studio lighting, no fill light, no posed photography, no clean commercial look, no saturated primaries, no Miami Vice palette, no fashion photography aesthetics, no sharp digital sharpening, no modern color grading, no HDR.

presets.name: Modernist interior
presets.text: Robby Müller, To Live and Die in L.A. 1985, 35mm spherical 1.85:1, Arriflex 35 BL3, Fuji A125T 8511 tungsten negative. Dark interior, floor-to-ceiling industrial grid windows, exterior blows to cool blue-white. Single illuminated subject or object as compositional pin in foreground. Zero attempt to balance exposure. Interior darkness accepted completely. Locked off. Architecture and shadow do the work.
presets.neg: No balanced exposure, no fill light, no warm interior lighting, no cozy atmosphere, no luxury interior aesthetics, no sharp detail in shadows, no HDR shadow recovery.

presets.name: Night desert road
presets.text: Robby Müller, To Live and Die in L.A. 1985, 35mm spherical 1.85:1, Arriflex 35 BL3, Fuji A125T 8511 tungsten negative. Car-mounted low angle shot on road surface. Double yellow lines as vanishing perspective. Deep indigo sky, not black, not purple, actual indigo. Near zero shadow detail. Small amber road markers only warm tones. Organic grain visible. Total commitment to darkness.
presets.neg: No bright moonlight, no dramatic lighting, no colorful night sky, no neon, no city glow, no HDR night photography, no purple sky, no lifted shadows.

presets.name: The Threshold
presets.text: Robby Müller, To Live and Die in L.A. 1985, 35mm spherical 1.85:1, Arriflex 35 BL3, Fuji A125T 8511 tungsten negative. Two opposing practical light sources, deep saturated red ambient from one direction, cool green point source from opposite. Glass partition divides color zones. Skin shifts to sickly green or deep red by position. Shadows absolute black. Maximum saturation only within source zones. Dangerous, transactional.
presets.neg: No cyberpunk aesthetics, no neon sign look, no nightclub photography, no pink or purple tones, no blue tones, no neutral white light, no clean skin tones, no beauty lighting.

presets.name: Desert exterior
presets.text: Robby Müller, To Live and Die in L.A. 1985, 35mm spherical 1.85:1, Arriflex 35 BL3, Fuji A125T 8511 tungsten negative. Joshua Tree terrain. Desert reads pale and bleached not warm and golden, cool lavender-rose palette. Deep focus, horizon sharp. Near pastel color rendering. Sun harsh, flat, merciless. No romanticization of landscape.
presets.neg: No warm golden desert tones, no romantic landscape photography, no travel photography aesthetics, no saturated blue sky, no golden hour, no warm orange sand.

presets.name: Dusk portrait
presets.text: Robby Müller, To Live and Die in L.A. 1985, 35mm spherical 1.85:1, Arriflex 35 BL3, Fuji A125T 8511 tungsten negative. Expose for sky, purple to amber gradient. Face falls to near darkness, one catch light in the eye only. Face reads as texture not portrait. Sky does the storytelling. Subject secondary to atmosphere.
presets.neg: No beauty portrait lighting, no fill light on face, no reflector, no golden hour warmth on skin, no glamour, no sharp facial detail, no studio portrait look.

presets.name: Warehouse silhouette
presets.text: Robby Müller, To Live and Die in L.A. 1985, 35mm spherical 1.85:1, Arriflex 35 BL3, Fuji A125T 8511 tungsten negative. Dark interior, single door or bay opening as only light source. Figure as pure silhouette or near-silhouette in doorframe. 90% of frame can be absolute black, commit to it. Corrugated metal, concrete surfaces. The door is the light.
presets.neg: No fill light, no ambient room light, no lifted shadows, no detail in dark areas, no HDR, no multiple light sources, no warm interior.

presets.name: Pursuit
presets.text: Robby Müller, To Live and Die in L.A. 1985, 35mm spherical 1.85:1, Arriflex 35 BL3, Fuji A125T 8511 tungsten negative. Midday flat California sun, no shadows, bleached palette. Subject vehicle in hard lateral motion, rear wheel lift or tire smoke, organic motion blur. Camera locked or near-locked on follow vehicle. Chaos belongs to subject not lens. No hero framing. Documentary witness.
presets.neg: No heroic framing, no dramatic lighting, no golden hour, no cinematic lens flare, no Michael Bay aesthetics, no sharp CGI quality, no video game render.

layers.name: Negative scan
layers.text: Processing: Clean 2K scan of original camera negative, flat log color space, no digital noise reduction, no sharpening. Grain structure fully resolved, original silver halide crystals present. The pure photochemical record before any human interpretation. Maximum shadow and highlight detail. Color is neutral and unmanaged.
layers.neg: No color grading, no timing, no sharpening, no noise reduction, no crushed blacks, no saturated color, no contrast push, no stylization.

layers.name: Release print
layers.text: Processing: Theatrical release print, third or fourth generation from original negative. Greens push slightly yellow, reds become brick and muted, blues cool further toward gray. Contrast built through duplication generations. Grain coarser and more uniform. Gate weave, image breathes. This is the version someone saw in a theater.
layers.neg: No clean digital transfer, no modern restoration, no noise reduction, no sharpening, no HDR, no Dolby Vision grade.

layers.name: Telecine
layers.text: Processing: Telecine transfer, film-to-tape, CRT monitor color space. Slight video softness on top of film grain, two textures fighting each other. Contrast reduced for television broadcast, blacks lifted to legal video levels. Flesh tones push toward video warmth. This is how a generation remembered this film.
layers.neg: No clean digital image, no modern transfer, no 4K restoration, no sharp detail, no Blu-ray quality.

layers.name: Broadcast transfer
layers.text: Processing: Analog broadcast transmission, cable or UHF, mid-1980s to early 1990s. The image has traveled through multiple tape generations and transmission stages. Noise floor rises noticeably in dark areas — grain and video noise coexist. Color saturation compresses further, reds and skin tones bleed slightly at edges. Slight temporal warble from tape generation loss. Contrast is flat, almost milky in the highlights. This is the version someone caught accidentally at 11pm on USA Network, half asleep on the couch, and never forgot.
layers.neg: No clean image, no sharp detail, no modern transfer, no film grain without video noise, no pure blacks, no saturated color, no HDR, no digital clarity of any kind.
